name: Alcoa Municipal Employees
description_short: Alcoa Municipal Employees Federal Credit Union is a small, well-capitalized credit union chartered in 1971, serving municipal employees and government workers in Alcoa, Tennessee with basic deposit and lending services.
description_long:
Alcoa Municipal Employees Federal Credit Union was established in 1971 and holds Charter Number 20513 from the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA). The credit union is headquartered in Alcoa, Tennessee, and serves a membership base of 1,057 individuals, primarily federal, state, and local government employees. As of Q1 2024, the institution maintains total assets of $4.61 million, positioning it in the smallest peer group (less than $2 million to less than $10 million in assets).

The credit union offers traditional financial services including member deposit accounts and loan products. As of Q1 2024, the institution held $3.94 million in total shares and deposits from members and maintained a loan portfolio of $2.37 million. The credit union generates revenue through interest income on loans and modest fee income, which totaled $475 in Q1 2024. Leadership is provided by CEO Denise S. Jeandron, and the institution operates with minimal staffing: one full-time employee and one part-time employee.

What distinguishes Alcoa Municipal EFCU is its "well-capitalized" status with a net worth ratio of 13.94—significantly above the 7.0 threshold required for well-capitalized classification. This indicates strong capital reserves relative to its asset base. The credit union maintains NCUA insurance protection for member deposits and operates with conservative financial practices, as evidenced by zero non-interest expenses related to credit loss and minimal borrowings.

However, this is an extremely small institution with very limited infrastructure. It operates from a single branch location with no ATM network, making access dependent on in-person visits or online services. The minimal staffing means service availability may be constrained. For prospective members, this credit union is best suited as a secondary account or for those with deep local ties to municipal employment in the Alcoa area rather than as a primary financial institution. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Member deposit accounts (shares and savings deposits totaling $3.94M)
Auto loans (mortgage and auto loan products mentioned, with historical trend tracking available)
Wire transfer and payment services (implied through check-cashing category reference)
Limited fee-based services (overdraft fees, ATM fees, credit card fees totaling $475 in Q1 2024)
Online account access (credit union routing number provided for electronic transactions)
pros: Well-capitalized with net worth ratio of 13.94, significantly above regulatory minimums, indicating strong financial stability
Established institution chartered in 1971 with 50+ years of operational history
NCUA-insured deposits protect member funds up to applicable limits
Serves defined membership of municipal and government employees with aligned field of membership
Generated positive net income of $27,283 in Q1 2024 despite small size
Low non-interest expenses ($37,150 in Q1 2024) suggesting lean, efficient operations
No outstanding borrowings as of Q1 2024, indicating conservative debt management
cons: Extremely small institution with only 1,057 members and minimal staffing (1 full-time, 1 part-time employee), severely limiting service availability and responsiveness
Single branch location in Alcoa, TN with zero ATMs, providing no geographic convenience or 24/7 cash access
Limited loan portfolio of $2.37 million may restrict lending capacity and product offerings for members with substantial credit needs
Very small fee income ($475 in Q1 2024) and minimal non-interest revenue suggest limited ancillary services and products
Eligibility restricted to federal, state, and local government employees—not open to general public membership
best_for: Municipal employees in the Alcoa, Tennessee area seeking a local credit union with government employee focus
Members requiring only basic deposit services and small personal loans with minimal banking needs
Individuals with existing long-term relationships to the credit union seeking continued membership
diagnosis: Alcoa Municipal EFCU is best for existing government employees in the immediate Alcoa area with simple banking needs and ties to the institution, seeking a financially stable local credit union. The main caveat is the severe operational constraints—single location, no ATMs, minimal staffing, and restricted membership eligibility—making this impractical as a primary bank for most consumers; it functions primarily as a secondary account for members already embedded in the municipal employment network.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
